finding: storage/manager.rs + utils/magic.rs + utils/zipbomb.rs moved BYTE-FOR-BYTE (diff vs pre-move blob = 0 changed lines). storage/{mod,filesystem}.rs, types.rs, permissions.rs differ by ONLY their single import-path swap (2 lines each). No behavioural change.

FileRepository: every RETAINED method's SQL query text is byte-identical to the pre-move blob (import swaps + inline-const + removal of the 2 workflow_run_id helpers are the only diffs). query!/query_as! row shapes unchanged → compile-time verification identical. cargo check -p ziee-file = 0 against the file-only build DB proves the queries verify.

N7/STOP-risk#3: golden regen (both binaries, DB ziee_build_12381b49) → types.ui.ts + types.desktop.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL; openapi.ui.json + openapi.desktop.json CANONICALLY-EQUAL vs .extraction/baseline. The File.* aide operations move behind a shim but routes.rs/handlers/*_docs stay in ziee unchanged, so the emitted spec is invariant. Generated files git-checkout-reverted after.

Base files/file_versions schema moved to the crate (original filename+version preserved → merged _sqlx_migrations slot unchanged). workflow_run_id col + idx dropped (N9). Merged composition proven: ziee's build.rs globbed sdk/crates/ziee-file/migrations + workflow/202607144231 + edited file_fkeys into ziee_build_12381b49; cargo check -p ziee = 0 (all file query! verify) and the DB shows files WITHOUT workflow_run_id + file_workflow_runs present.

EA-schema fingerprint (schema_fp.sql, tab-sep) vs baseline: the ONLY store-attributable delta is REMOVED {files.workflow_run_id col, its FK, its partial index} and ADDED {file_workflow_runs table + 2 FKs + PK + index}. (Other added tables — magic_link_tokens/desktop_settings/remote_access_settings/host_mounts/host_mount_policy — are desktop-schema present because desktop generate-openapi ran against the shared DB; grep -c confirms they are ABSENT from the server-only baseline, i.e. not this chunk's change.) The file↔run relationship is preserved by the join → functionally equivalent per RESOLVED.

Permission model: the 6 files::* keys moved with STRINGS unchanged (files::read/upload/download/delete/preview/generate_token) and now impl ziee_identity::PermissionCheck (== ziee's re-exported trait), so ziee's RequirePermissions<(FilesUpload,)> extractor + with_permission gating in the retained handlers bind identically. The Users-group GRANT migration stays ziee (N9 — domain perms must not live in the SDK). No perm widened/narrowed. Upload-security validators (magic sniff / zipbomb) moved byte-identical — no weakening of MIME-smuggling / zip-bomb defenses.

download_with_token's revocation re-check (Repos.user.get_by_id + is_active + get_user_groups + check_permission_union on files::download) is UNCHANGED — the whole handler stays ziee. The download-token signer (iss/aud=ziee-download) also stays ziee (config.rs holds JwtConfig). Deliberately NOT moved to the SDK to avoid re-implementing the identity re-check behind a resolver seam this chunk; documented as the ziee-file-http follow-up. No regression: the security-critical path is byte-identical.

FileProcessor severs ProcessingManager: ziee's ZieeFileProcessor::process = ProcessingManager::new().process_file(bytes,mime) (returns Result<ProcessingResult,AppError>); the SDK ingest_bytes does the warn-on-err→default degrade exactly as the pre-move ingest_bytes. FileEvents severs sync: ZieeFileEvents::on_file_changed/on_file_deleted route to publish_file_changed_with_origin/publish_file_deleted_with_origin (SyncEntity::File, owner-scoped) — same events, same audience. The SDK never names ProcessingManager, SyncEntity, or file_rag.

A5 run-delete cascade preserved: previously files.workflow_run_id FK was ON DELETE SET NULL and A5 EXPLICITLY listed+deleted run-created files. Now list_ids_by_workflow_run→Repos.file_workflow_runs.list_file_ids (same run-scoped id set), files deleted explicitly BEFORE the run delete; the join rows carry ON DELETE CASCADE so a run delete auto-clears residual links (a file the cascade kept loses only its link, matching the old SET NULL semantics). resource_link A3 linking rewired to .link (idempotent ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).

grep across the crate: zero references to workflow_runs / chat / llm / project / memory / file_rag / SyncEntity / ProcessingManager. Only couplings are ziee_core (AppError/ApiResult), ziee_identity (PermissionCheck), and the two declared seams. N9 clean boundary — the store is fully domain-agnostic.

ziee-file carries a build.rs (mirrors ziee-auth) provisioning ziee_file_build_<key> from its own migrations/ for the query! macros. Proven: standalone cargo check -p ziee-file = 0 (build DB ziee_file_build_c9fba9b4 auto-provisioned). The base files/file_versions have no cross-module FK (the users/self FKs live in the ziee-retained file_fkeys), so the file subset migrates standalone.

The ~59 external store consumers (code_sandbox, tools/files, files_mcp, mcp/resource_link, workflow, file_rag, llm_provider_files, user-delete, chat/core/export, core/repository Repos.file) compile UNCHANGED behind the re-export shim (pub use ziee_file::{models,permissions,storage,types}+FileRepository+init_file_storage; utils re-exports extension_of/magic/zipbomb; processing re-exports ProcessingResult). cargo check -p ziee = 0 and -p ziee-desktop = 0 prove no consumer broke. files_mcp (the both-halves consumer): its store bits resolve to ziee_file via the shim, its processing bits (ProcessingManager/pandoc) stay ziee-local — thin shim held.

All three gates green: cargo check -p ziee = 0, cargo check -p ziee-desktop = 0, cd sdk && cargo check --workspace = 0. Zero unused-import/dead-code errors in the touched crates (two initial unused imports — the redundant `repository` shim re-export + the now-unused ProcessingMetadata import in processing/mod.rs — were removed).
